name: Remove Outermost Parentheses
description: A valid parentheses string s is primitive if it is nonempty, and there does not exist a way to split it into s = A + B, with A and B nonempty valid parentheses strings. Given a valid parentheses string s, consider its primitive decomposition. Return s after removing the outermost parentheses of every primitive string in the primitive decomposition of s.
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 10^5. s is a valid parentheses string. s[i] is either '(' or ')'.

performance.stress_intent: exercise primitive decomposition and nesting-depth tracking on three max-length valid parenthesis strings that reject quadratic output concatenation locally
